title: Intro
text: Welcome to Science Radar, your weekly digest of the most surprising, frontier-pushing, and cross-disciplinary discoveries from across all of science. This week we've found papers that challenge long-held assumptions, open doors to entirely new research directions, and connect ideas across fields in ways nobody expected. Let's dive in.

title: This Week's Highlights
text: And now, a quick tour through this week's other noteworthy papers.

title: Automated genome mining predicts structural diversity and taxonomic distribution of peptide metallophores across bacteria
text: Automated genome mining predicts structural diversity and taxonomic distribution of peptide metallophores across bacteria — eLife. Microbial competition for trace metals shapes their communities and interactions with humans and plants. Many bacteria scavenge trace metals with metallophores, small molecules that chelate environmental metal ions. Metallophore production may be predicted by genome mining, where genomes are scanned

title: Descending locus coeruleus noradrenergic signaling to spinal astrocyte subset is required for stress-induced mechanical pain hypersensitivity
text: Descending locus coeruleus noradrenergic signaling to spinal astrocyte subset is required for stress-induced mechanical pain hypersensitivity — eLife. It is known that stress powerfully alters pain, but its underlying mechanisms remain elusive. Here, we identified a circuit, locus coeruleus descending noradrenergic neurons projecting to the spinal dorsal horn (LC<sup>→SDH</sup>-NA neurons), that is activated by acute exposure to restraint stress a

text:
Linear anti-jamming algorithm design for multi-channel optical fiber communication — PLOS ONE.

by Haichao Yu

title: Comparative efficacy of different doses of mesenchymal stem cells derived from different tissue sources for knee osteoarthritis: a systematic review and network meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
text:
Comparative efficacy of different doses of mesenchymal stem cells derived from different tissue sources for knee osteoarthritis: a systematic review and network meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials — PeerJ. Introduction

title: A GABAergic network from AVP- to VIP-neurons in the suprachiasmatic nucleus sets the timing of circadian behavior rhythms

title: Outro
text: That's Science Radar for this week. Every one of these papers is a reminder that the most interesting science often lives at the edges — where fields collide, where assumptions break, and where the next big question is just starting to take shape. Until next week, keep questioning everything.
